name: Babson College
conference: New England Women's and Men's Athletic Conference (NEWMAC)
notes: Business-focused liberal arts college with strong entrepreneurship culture. NEWMAC conference. Competitive regional program.

name: Clark University
conference: New England Women's and Men's Athletic Conference (NEWMAC)
notes: Research university in Worcester with a liberal arts core. NEWMAC conference. Solid mid-level D3 program.

name: Gordon College
notes: Christian liberal arts college north of Boston. CCC conference. Growing soccer program with faith-based community.

name: Springfield College
notes: Historically focused on health sciences and physical education — birthplace of basketball. Strong athletic culture and competitive soccer program.

name: University of Massachusetts Dartmouth
notes: Public university in southeastern Massachusetts. Little East Conference. Affordable D3 option with solid soccer history.

name: University of Massachusetts Boston
notes: Urban public university on Boston Harbor. Commuter-heavy campus. D3 soccer in the Little East Conference.

name: Bridgewater State University
notes: Public university south of Boston. MASCAC conference. Competitive D3 program with accessible admissions.

name: Roger Williams University
notes: Scenic campus on Narragansett Bay. CCC conference. Strong architecture and law programs alongside competitive soccer.

name: Salve Regina University
notes: Catholic university in historic Newport. CCC conference. Beautiful oceanfront campus with a close-knit athletic community.

name: Eastern Connecticut State University
notes: Public liberal arts university in Connecticut. Little East Conference. Historically competitive men's soccer program with NCAA tournament appearances.

name: University of Southern Maine
notes: Public university in Portland, Maine's largest city. Little East Conference. Affordable option for New England students.

name: SUNY Geneseo
conference: State University of New York Athletic Conference (SUNYAC)
notes: One of the most selective SUNY schools. Strong academic reputation and competitive SUNYAC soccer program.

name: SUNY Oswego
conference: State University of New York Athletic Conference (SUNYAC)
notes: Public university on Lake Ontario. SUNYAC conference. Active campus life and solid D3 soccer program.

name: SUNY New Paltz
conference: State University of New York Athletic Conference (SUNYAC)
notes: Scenic campus in the Hudson Valley. Strong arts and liberal arts programs. Competitive SUNYAC soccer.

name: SUNY Brockport
conference: State University of New York Athletic Conference (SUNYAC)
notes: Known as a strong athletic school in SUNYAC. Consistently competitive soccer program with NCAA tournament history.

name: SUNY Plattsburgh
conference: State University of New York Athletic Conference (SUNYAC)
notes: Public university near the Canadian border on Lake Champlain. SUNYAC conference. Good value with solid soccer history.

name: St. John Fisher University
notes: Catholic college near Rochester. Empire 8 conference. Strong pre-professional programs and competitive soccer.

name: Utica University
notes: Comprehensive university in central New York. Empire 8 conference. Welcoming campus with solid athletic programs.

name: Nazareth University
notes: Liberal arts college in suburban Rochester. Empire 8 conference. Known for health sciences and education programs.

name: Alfred University
notes: Small university known for ceramics, art, and engineering. Rural campus in western New York. Empire 8 conference.

name: Hartwick College
notes: Small liberal arts college with a storied soccer tradition. Liberty League. Men's program has made multiple NCAA tournament appearances.

name: Hobart and William Smith Colleges
notes: Coordinate colleges on Seneca Lake. Liberty League. Hobart men's lacrosse is elite; soccer is competitive with strong culture.

name: St. Lawrence University
notes: Liberal arts university near the Canadian border. Liberty League. Strong outdoor and athletic culture in the North Country.

name: Union College
notes: One of the oldest liberal arts colleges in the US. Liberty League. Strong STEM focus with competitive D3 soccer.

name: Drew University
notes: Small liberal arts university in suburban New Jersey, 30 miles from NYC. Centennial Conference.

name: The College of New Jersey
notes: Flagship public liberal arts college of NJ. NJAC conference. One of the strongest D3 programs in the Northeast — consistent NCAA tournament contender.

name: Montclair State University
notes: Large public university near NYC. NJAC conference. Nationally competitive D3 soccer program with multiple NCAA titles.

name: Ramapo College of New Jersey
notes: Public liberal arts college in northern NJ near the Ramapo Mountains. NJAC conference. Scenic campus, solid soccer program.

name: Kean University
notes: Public metropolitan university in Union County. NJAC conference. Accessible admissions with growing athletic programs.

name: Rutgers University–Camden
notes: Urban campus of Rutgers across from Philadelphia. NJAC conference. Improving athletic culture under Rutgers brand.

name: Fairleigh Dickinson University–Florham
notes: D3 campus of FDU on a former estate in Morris County. Middle Atlantic Conference. Suburban NJ campus with solid soccer.

name: Albright College
notes: Liberal arts college in Reading, PA. Middle Atlantic Conference. Known for fashion and business programs alongside solid athletics.

name: Ursinus College
notes: Liberal arts college near Philadelphia. Centennial Conference. Academically rigorous with a competitive soccer tradition.

name: Lebanon Valley College
notes: Small Methodist-affiliated college in central PA. MAC conference. Known for physical therapy and music programs.

name: Elizabethtown College
notes: Church of the Brethren-affiliated liberal arts college in Lancaster County. MAC conference. Strong occupational therapy program.

name: Moravian University
notes: One of the oldest colleges in the US, Moravian heritage. Bethlehem, PA. Landmark Conference with growing soccer program.

name: Susquehanna University
notes: Lutheran-affiliated liberal arts university on the Susquehanna River. Landmark Conference. Strong communications and business programs.

name: Goucher College
notes: Liberal arts college near Baltimore with mandatory study abroad. Landmark Conference. Small, diverse campus community.

name: Hood College
notes: Predominantly women's college that went coed. Frederick, MD. MAC conference. Strong biomedical sciences programs.

name: McDaniel College
notes: Liberal arts college in Carroll County, Maryland. Centennial Conference. Known for social work and education programs.

name: Salisbury University
notes: Public university on the Eastern Shore. Capital Athletic Conference. One of the elite D3 programs nationally — multiple NCAA tournament runs and championship-caliber rosters.

name: York College of Pennsylvania
notes: Career-focused liberal arts college in south-central PA. MAC conference. Strong nursing and business programs.

name: Randolph-Macon College
notes: Methodist-affiliated liberal arts college near Richmond, VA. ODAC conference. Close campus community with solid soccer tradition.

name: Roanoke College
notes: Lutheran-affiliated liberal arts college in the Roanoke Valley. ODAC conference. Competitive soccer in a strong athletic conference.

name: Bridgewater College
notes: Church of the Brethren-affiliated college in the Shenandoah Valley. ODAC conference. Strong community atmosphere.

name: Virginia Wesleyan University
notes: Methodist-affiliated university in coastal Virginia. ODAC conference. Beachside location with solid athletic programs.

name: Guilford College
notes: Quaker-founded liberal arts college. ODAC conference. Known for social justice focus and inclusive campus culture.

name: Methodist University
notes: Methodist-affiliated university near Fort Bragg. USA South conference. Strong PGA golf management program.

name: Millsaps College
notes: Small liberal arts college in Jackson, MS. Southern Athletic Association. Strong academics with merit aid for student-athletes.

name: Rhodes College
notes: Highly selective liberal arts college in Memphis. Southern Athletic Association. Academically rigorous — often compared to Sewanee and Millsaps for D3 academic prestige in the South.

name: University of the South (Sewanee)
notes: Episcopal liberal arts university on a mountain plateau. Southern Athletic Association. Beautiful campus, strong writing and classics tradition.

name: Centre College
notes: Elite small liberal arts college in Kentucky. Southern Athletic Association. Highly selective with strong academic reputation and competitive D3 soccer.

name: Transylvania University
notes: One of the oldest universities in the US, in Lexington KY. HCAC conference. Known for biology and pre-med with solid soccer program.

name: John Carroll University
notes: Jesuit liberal arts university near Cleveland. Ohio Athletic Conference. Strong pre-professional programs and competitive OAC soccer.

name: Ohio Northern University
notes: United Methodist university in rural northwestern Ohio. OAC conference. Top pharmacy school; strong STEM focus with solid athletics.

name: Heidelberg University
notes: Small liberal arts university in Tiffin, OH. OAC conference. Close-knit campus with solid athletic programs.

name: Capital University
notes: Lutheran-affiliated university in suburban Columbus. OAC conference. Good location with strong business and law programs.

name: University of Mount Union
notes: Dominant D3 football school, but soccer is also competitive. OAC conference. Strong athletic culture overall.

name: Adrian College
notes: Methodist-affiliated liberal arts college in southeastern Michigan. MIAA conference. Close-knit campus with broad athletic offerings.

name: Alma College
notes: Presbyterian-affiliated liberal arts college in central Michigan. MIAA conference. Strong Scottish heritage and solid academic programs.

name: Albion College
notes: Methodist-affiliated liberal arts college in south-central Michigan. MIAA conference. Competitive soccer with strong academic culture.

name: Kalamazoo College
notes: Highly selective liberal arts college with a unique K-Plan (internship/study abroad). MIAA conference. Academically rigorous with competitive soccer.

name: Olivet College
notes: Small liberal arts college with a responsibility-based education model. MIAA conference. Accessible admissions.

name: University of Wisconsin–Eau Claire
notes: Public university known for strong academics and a vibrant campus. WIAC conference — one of the top D3 athletic conferences in the country.

name: University of Wisconsin–La Crosse
notes: Top-ranked public WIAC school with consistently competitive soccer. Beautiful blufftop campus on the Mississippi River. Multiple NCAA tournament appearances.

name: University of Wisconsin–Oshkosh
notes: Public WIAC university with a strong athletic tradition. Oshkosh regularly competes for WIAC soccer titles.

name: University of Wisconsin–Platteville
notes: Public engineering-focused university in southwest Wisconsin. WIAC conference. Competitive D3 soccer program.

name: University of Wisconsin–River Falls
notes: Public university near the Twin Cities. WIAC conference. Strong agriculture and teacher education programs.

name: University of Wisconsin–Stevens Point
notes: Public WIAC university known for natural resources and sustainability. Competitive D3 soccer with strong environmental programs.

name: University of Wisconsin–Stout
notes: Wisconsin's polytechnic university with career-focused programs. WIAC conference. Solid D3 soccer program.

name: University of Wisconsin–Whitewater
notes: WIAC powerhouse — known for elite D3 football, but soccer is also strong. Nationally competitive program with significant athletic tradition.

name: Augustana College
conference: College Conference of Illinois and Wisconsin (CCIW)
notes: Lutheran liberal arts college on the Rock Island/Moline Quad Cities. CCIW conference. Strong academics and competitive soccer.

name: Illinois Wesleyan University
conference: College Conference of Illinois and Wisconsin (CCIW)
notes: Selective liberal arts university in Bloomington, IL. CCIW conference. Strong fine arts and sciences with competitive soccer.

name: North Central College
conference: College Conference of Illinois and Wisconsin (CCIW)
notes: United Methodist university in affluent Naperville suburb of Chicago. CCIW conference. Competitive soccer with strong business and engineering programs.

name: Wartburg College
notes: Lutheran liberal arts college in Waverly, IA. Iowa Conference. Historically strong D3 soccer with multiple NCAA tournament appearances.

name: Luther College
notes: Lutheran liberal arts college in the scenic Driftless Area. Iowa Conference. World-renowned music program alongside competitive soccer.

name: University of Dubuque
notes: Presbyterian-affiliated university in Dubuque, IA. Iowa Conference. Aviation and business programs are popular alongside competitive soccer.

name: Simpson College
notes: Methodist-affiliated liberal arts college near Des Moines. Iowa Conference. Close-knit campus with solid athletic programs.

name: Central College
notes: Reformed Church-affiliated liberal arts college in Pella, IA. Iowa Conference. Competitive soccer program with strong Dutch heritage community.

name: Hamline University
notes: Methodist-affiliated liberal arts university in St. Paul. MIAC conference. Urban location with close ties to Twin Cities community.

name: Bethel University
notes: Evangelical Covenant Christian university near St. Paul. MIAC conference. Strong faith community with competitive soccer.

name: Concordia College
notes: Lutheran liberal arts college in Moorhead, MN. MIAC conference. Known for world-class choral music and solid soccer.

name: Pacific University
notes: Private university west of Portland. Northwest Conference. Optometry and health sciences are top programs; strong D3 soccer history.

name: George Fox University
notes: Quaker-affiliated Christian university south of Portland. Northwest Conference. Competitive D3 soccer in a strong regional conference.

name: Pacific Lutheran University
notes: Lutheran university near Tacoma. Northwest Conference. One of the top D3 soccer programs in the Pacific Northwest — multiple NCAA tournament appearances.

name: Whitworth University
notes: Presbyterian-affiliated liberal arts university in Spokane. Northwest Conference. Consistently one of the top D3 programs nationally with multiple Final Four and championship appearances.

name: Whitman College
notes: Highly selective liberal arts college in eastern Washington's wine country. Northwest Conference. Academic prestige rivals top NESCAC schools.

name: Lewis & Clark College
notes: Selective liberal arts college in Portland. Northwest Conference. Known for environmental studies, law school, and competitive soccer.

name: Linfield University
notes: Baptist-affiliated liberal arts university in Oregon wine country. Northwest Conference. Nationally competitive D3 football; soccer program is also strong.

name: Willamette University
notes: Oldest university on the West Coast, in Salem, OR. Northwest Conference. Selective liberal arts with competitive D3 soccer and law school on campus.

name: University of Dallas
notes: Catholic liberal arts university near Dallas. SCAC conference. Rigorous classical core curriculum; strong soccer program in the Southwest.

name: Austin College
notes: Presbyterian-affiliated liberal arts college in Sherman, TX. SCAC conference. Pre-law and pre-med focus with solid soccer program.

name: Texas Lutheran University
notes: Lutheran-affiliated liberal arts university near San Antonio. SCAC conference. Small campus with competitive D3 soccer.

name: LaGrange College
notes: Methodist-affiliated liberal arts college in western Georgia. USA South conference. Small, intimate campus near Atlanta.

name: Berry College
notes: Beautiful liberal arts college on the largest contiguous college campus in the world, near Rome, GA. Southern Athletic Association. Strong work ethic culture and competitive soccer.

name: Oglethorpe University
notes: Small liberal arts college in suburban Atlanta. USA South conference. Great location in one of the fastest-growing cities in the South.

name: Colorado College
notes: Highly selective liberal arts college with the unique Block Plan (one course at a time). Rocky Mountain setting. Academically elite D3 with competitive soccer.
